item.text: The baseline confirms level 1 is untouched. I’m now using the permitted observation surface to summarize the initial scene and test every advertised action on independent clones; outputs will be component bounding boxes and localized deltas rather than frame dumps.

The probes identify the avatar as the color-6 ring at the left of the upper chamber. Up/down move it by one six-pixel lane; right/left extend and retract its color-1 tether. Contact automatically attaches a colored block, and retraction drags it. The lower chamber is a static order cue (`8 → 14 → 9`); touching the first correct block marks its cue, giving a useful dense progress signal. I’m verifying the full retrieve-and-consume cycle and whether vertical movement preserves attachment.

The placement mechanics are now coherent: an adjacent first token travels with the avatar; extending pushes the growing horizontal train, contact adds the next token, and retracting restores the train. Vertical movement carries the attached train. This supports the lower cue as an exact ordered train target. I’m testing the shortest full composition: build `[8,14]`, carry it to the `9` lane, then add `9`.

The clone reaches reward 1 in 26 actions. I’ve encoded that exact verified sequence using three reusable mechanics-only legs—vertical lane movement, tether extension, and tether retraction—while keeping all level-specific composition in `play_level_1`. I’m validating it now through the real harness and replay checker.
Validation passed: `levels=1`, `moves=26`, `replay_ok=True`, with no error. I’m removing only the temporary probe scripts I created; the reusable library and player implementation remain.

Implemented level 1 using reusable legs only:

- [legs.py](/Users/sasha/gkm/arc/crack_lab/runs/scratch/gkm_legs_ws_sk48_cheap_high_screen/legs.py:4): vertical movement, tether extension, and retraction legs.
- [players.py](/Users/sasha/gkm/arc/crack_lab/runs/scratch/gkm_legs_ws_sk48_cheap_high_screen/players.py:5): composes the verified `8 → 14 → 9` collection sequence.
